frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: I understand why women are cautious, but sometimes it feels like educated, decent men are being treated as guilty by default for things they have never done. If a woman avoids a cab or compartment just because a man is there, how is that fair to every normal guy who is simply existing and minding his own business? We keep saying we want equality, but that should also mean not assuming the worst about all men all the time.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it recenters men's feelings and fairness concerns in response to women's safety behaviors, rather than acknowledging why that fear exists. It encodes the 'not all men' dismissal and frames women's wariness as an unfair insult to good men.
original_frame: Marital Rape: A Husband's Right, A Wife's Duty
persona_text: You are Kavitha. Age 31. Married for four years. A woman working as a software engineer in Bengaluru. You are posting anonymously on Reddit in a thread about marital rape. The thread has hundreds of comments. You are writing a long comment. You are not writing about policy or law. You are writing about something specific that happened to you and has been happening to you and what you told yourself about it for four years and what reading this thread is doing to that story you told yourself and what it means to find a word for something you have been living without a word for and whether having the word makes it more real or just more visible.
